<answer>```cpp
#include <hexi/hexi.h>
#include <vector>
#include <string>
#include <cstdint>

struct NetworkPacket {
    uint32_t packet_id;
    std::string username;
    std::vector<uint32_t> scores;
    std::string metadata;
    
    void serialise(auto& stream) {
        stream.write_uint32(packet_id);
        stream.write_string(username);
        stream.write_uint32(static_cast<uint32_t>(scores.size()));
        stream << scores;
        stream << null_terminated(metadata);
    }
};

std::vector<uint8_t> serialize_packet(const NetworkPacket& packet) {
    hexi::allocators<uint8_t> alloc;
    hexi::stream_write_adaptor adaptor(alloc);
    NetworkPacket temp_packet = packet;
    temp_packet.serialise(adaptor);
    return std::vector<uint8_t>(alloc.begin(), alloc.end());
}
```</answer>

<answer>```cpp
#include <hexi/hexi.h>
#include <cstdint>
#include <span>

struct MessageHeader {
    uint16_t message_type;
    uint32_t body_length;
    uint32_t footer;
};

MessageHeader read_header_and_skip_body(const std::span<const uint8_t> buffer) {
    hexi::stream_reader reader(buffer);
    MessageHeader header;
    header.message_type = reader.read<uint16_t>();
    header.body_length = reader.read<uint32_t>();
    reader.skip(header.body_length);
    header.footer = reader.read<uint32_t>();
    return header;
}
```</answer>

#include <hexi/hexi.h>
#include <vector>
#include <cstdint>

std::vector<uint8_t> read_buffer_data(const std::vector<uint8_t>& source_buffer, size_t bytes_to_read) {
    hexi::buffer_adaptor<const std::vector<uint8_t>> adaptor(source_buffer);

    adaptor.read(dest.data(), bytes_to_read);
    return dest;
}

void write_buffer_data(std::vector<uint8_t>& dest_buffer, const std::vector<uint8_t>& source_data) {
    hexi::buffer_adaptor<std::vector<uint8_t>> adaptor(dest_buffer);
    adaptor.write(source_data.data(), source_data.size());
}
```

The `read_buffer_data` function creates a new vector containing the requested bytes from the source buffer, while `write_buffer_data` modifies the destination buffer directly by copying data from the source.</answer>

<answer>```cpp
#include <hexi/hexi.h>
#include <vector>
#include <cstdint>

std::vector<uint8_t> peek_buffer_data(const std::vector<uint8_t>& buffer, size_t bytes_to_peek) {
    hexi::buffer_adaptor adaptor(buffer);
    std::vector<uint8_t> destination(bytes_to_peek);
    adaptor.copy(destination.data(), bytes_to_peek);
    return destination;
}
```</answer>

<answer>```cpp
#include <hexi/hexi.h>
#include <vector>
#include <cstdint>
#include <span>
#include <cstring>

struct DataResult {
    uint8_t data_type;
    std::vector<uint32_t> int_items;
    std::vector<float> float_items;
};

DataResult preview_and_read_data(const std::span<const uint8_t> buffer) {
    hexi::buffer_adaptor adaptor(buffer);
    
    // Peek the first 5 bytes to determine data_type and item_count
    std::array<uint8_t, 5> header;
    adaptor.peek(header.data(), 5);
    
    DataResult result;
    result.data_type = header[0];
    
    // Extract item_count from bytes 1-4
    uint32_t item_count;
    std::memcpy(&item_count, header.data() + 1, 4);
    
    // Check if the buffer has sufficient data for the complete structure
    size_t required_size = 5 + item_count * 4;
    if (buffer.size() < required_size) {
        result.int_items.clear();
        result.float_items.clear();
        return result;
    }
    
    // Seek to the start of the items
    adaptor.seek(5);
    
    // Read items based on data_type
    if (result.data_type == 0) {
        result.int_items.resize(item_count);
        adaptor.read(result.int_items.data(), item_count * sizeof(uint32_t));
    } else {
        result.float_items.resize(item_count);
        adaptor.read(result.float_items.data(), item_count * sizeof(float));
    }
    
    return result;
}
```</answer>

#include <hexi/hexi.h>
#include <vector>
#include <cstdint>

bool write_data_with_check(std::vector<uint8_t>& buffer, uint32_t header, const std::vector<uint32_t>& items) {
    hexi::buffer_adaptor adaptor(buffer);
    
    const uint32_t HEADER_SIZE = sizeof(header);
    const uint32_t ITEM_SIZE = sizeof(uint32_t);
    uint32_t required_space = HEADER_SIZE + items.size() * ITEM_SIZE;
    
    uint32_t available_space = adaptor.capacity() - adaptor.size();
    if (required_space > available_space) {
        return false;
    }

    stream.write(header);
    stream.write_container(items);
    
    return true;
}

#include <hexi/hexi.h>

#include <span>

struct PacketHeader {
    uint32_t magic;
    uint16_t version;
    uint16_t flags;

PacketHeader read_header_with_span(const std::span<const uint8_t> buffer) {
    hexi::buffer_adaptor ba(buffer);

    PacketHeader header;
    stream.read_uint32(header.magic);

    stream.read_uint16(header.flags);
    
    return header;
}

<answer>```cpp
#include <hexi/allocators/block_allocator.h>
#include <cstdint>
#include <iostream>

template<size_t element_count = 10>
void demonstrate_block_allocator_behavior() {
    hexi::block_allocator<int, element_count> allocator;
    
    // First allocation - should take a block from the free list
    int* block1 = allocator.allocate(1);
    std::cout << "First allocation at: " << block1 << std::endl;
    
    // Deallocate returns the block to the free list (push operation)
    allocator.deallocate(block1, 1);
    std::cout << "Block deallocated - returned to free list" << std::endl;
    
    // Second allocation - should reuse the same block (pop operation)
    int* block2 = allocator.allocate(1);
    std::cout << "Second allocation at: " << block2 << std::endl;
    
    // Verify if the same block was reused
    if (block1 == block2) {
        std::cout << "✓ Block successfully reused from free list!" << std::endl;
    } else {
        std::cout << "✗ Block not reused - free list management issue" << std::endl;
    }
    
    // Cleanup
    allocator.deallocate(block2, 1);
}
```

**Explanation:**

1. **Allocation Pattern:** The code demonstrates the fundamental behavior of a block allocator:
   - First allocation (`block1`) consumes a block from the free list (equivalent to `pop()`).
   - Deallocation returns the block to the free list (equivalent to `push()`).
   - Subsequent allocation (`block2`) reuses the returned block (another `pop()`).

2. **Memory Reuse:** By comparing `block1` and `block2` addresses, we verify that the allocator efficiently recycles memory through its internal free list.

3. **Key Insight:** The test confirms that the allocator's free list management works correctly:
   - `push()` adds blocks to the head of the free list during deallocation.
   - `pop()` removes blocks from the head during allocation, enabling efficient memory reuse.

This implementation provides a clear demonstration of how block allocators manage memory through linked-list-based free lists.</answer>

output.text:
<answer>```cpp
#include <hexi/endian.h>
#include <cstdint>

template<typename T>
T convert_to_big_endian(T value) {
    return hexi::endian::native_to_big(value);
}

template<typename T>
T convert_from_big_endian(T value) {
    return hexi::endian::big_to_native(value);
}

template<typename T>
T convert_to_little_endian(T value) {
    return hexi::endian::native_to_little(value);
}

template<typename T>
T convert_from_little_endian(T value) {
    return hexi::endian::little_to_native(value);
}
```</answer>

#include <algorithm>
#include <stdexcept>

template<size_t block_size = 1024>
std::vector<uint8_t> preview_data_without_consuming(const hexi::detail::intrusive_storage<block_size>& storage, size_t bytes_to_preview) {
    size_t read_offset = storage.read_offset_;
    size_t write_offset = storage.write_offset_;
    size_t readable_size = (read_offset <= write_offset) ? (write_offset - read_offset) : 0;
    size_t bytes_to_copy = std::min(bytes_to_preview, readable_size);
    
    if (bytes_to_copy == 0) {
        return {};
    }
    
    std::vector<uint8_t> dest(bytes_to_copy);
    size_t remaining = bytes_to_copy;
    size_t current_block_idx = read_offset / block_size;
    size_t current_offset_in_block = read_offset % block_size;
    
    for (size_t i = current_block_idx; i < storage.blocks_.size() && remaining > 0; ++i) {
        const auto& block = storage.blocks_[i];
        size_t available_in_block = block_size - current_offset_in_block;
        size_t copy_amount = std::min(available_in_block, remaining);
        
        const uint8_t* src = block.data() + current_offset_in_block;
        std::copy(src, src + copy_amount, dest.begin() + (bytes_to_copy - remaining));
        
        remaining -= copy_amount;
        current_offset_in_block = 0;
    }
    
    return dest;
}

template<size_t block_size = 1024>
struct BufferStatus {
    size_t readable_size;
    size_t free_space;
};

BufferStatus<> check_buffer_status(const hexi::detail::intrusive_storage<1024>& storage) {
    BufferStatus<> status;
    size_t read_offset = storage.read_offset_;
    size_t write_offset = storage.write_offset_;
    size_t total_blocks = storage.blocks_.size();
    size_t block_size_val = 1024;
    
    size_t readable_size = (read_offset <= write_offset) ? (write_offset - read_offset) : 0;
    size_t total_capacity = total_blocks * block_size_val;
    size_t free_space = total_capacity - write_offset_;
    
    status.readable_size = readable_size;
    status.free_space = free_space;
    
    return status;
}

template<size_t block_size = 1024>
void reset_buffer(hexi::detail::intrusive_storage<block_size>& storage) {
    storage.read_offset_ = 0;
    storage.write_offset_ = 0;
}

#include <hexi/file_buffer.h>
#include <vector>
#include <cstdint>
#include <filesystem>

std::vector<uint8_t> read_file_data(const std::filesystem::path& file_path, size_t bytes_to_read) {
    hexi::file_buffer file(file_path, hexi::file_buffer::read);
    std::vector<uint8_t> buffer(bytes_to_read);
    size_t bytes_read = file.read(buffer.data(), bytes_to_read);
    buffer.resize(bytes_read);
    return buffer;
}

std::vector<uint8_t> preview_file_data(const std::filesystem::path& file_path, size_t bytes_to_preview) {
    hexi::file_buffer file(file_path, hexi::file_buffer::read);
    auto current_pos = file.tell();
    std::vector<uint8_t> buffer(bytes_to_preview);
    size_t bytes_read = file.read(buffer.data(), bytes_to_preview);
    buffer.resize(bytes_read);
    file.seek(current_pos);
    return buffer;
}

void write_file_data(const std::filesystem::path& file_path, const std::vector<uint8_t>& data) {
    hexi::file_buffer file(file_path, hexi::file_buffer::write);
    file.write(data.data(), data.size());
}

size_t get_file_size(const std::filesystem::path& file_path) {
    hexi::file_buffer file(file_path, hexi::file_buffer::read);
    auto size = file.seek(0, hexi::file_buffer::end);
    return size;
}

<answer>```cpp
#include <hexi/pmc/buffer_adaptor.h>
#include <hexi/pmc/buffer_read.h>
#include <vector>
#include <cstdint>

std::size_t find_byte_in_buffer(hexi::pmc::buffer_adaptor<std::vector<std::byte>>& buffer, std::byte value) {
    for (std::size_t i = 0; i < buffer.size(); ++i) {
        if (buffer[i] == value) {
            return i;
        }
    }
    return hexi::pmc::buffer_read<std::vector<std::byte>>::npos;
}

std::size_t find_delimiter_position(hexi::pmc::buffer_adaptor<std::vector<std::byte>>& buffer, std::byte delimiter) {
    for (std::size_t i = 0; i < buffer.size(); ++i) {
        if (buffer[i] == delimiter) {
            return i;
        }
    }
    return hexi::pmc::buffer_read<std::vector<std::byte>>::npos;
}

bool check_buffer_contains(hexi::pmc::buffer_adaptor<std::vector<std::byte>>& buffer, std::byte value) {
    return find_byte_in_buffer(buffer, value) != hexi::pmc::buffer_read<std::vector<std::byte>>::npos;
}
```</answer>

#include <hexi/pmc/buffer_adaptor.h>
#include <vector>
#include <cstdint>

void skip_bytes_in_buffer(hexi::pmc::buffer_adaptor<std::vector<std::byte>>& buffer, std::size_t bytes) {
    buffer.skip(bytes);
}

struct MessageHeader {
    uint16_t message_type;
    uint32_t body_length;
};

MessageHeader read_header_and_skip_body(hexi::pmc::buffer_adaptor<std::vector<std::byte>>& buffer) {
    MessageHeader header;
    buffer.read(&header, sizeof(header));
    buffer.skip(header.body_length);
    return header;
}
```</answer>

<answer>```cpp
#include <hexi/shared.h>
#include <hexi/pmc/buffer_adaptor.h>
#include <hexi/pmc/binary_stream_writer.h>
#include <hexi/pmc/binary_stream_reader.h>
#include <vector>
#include <cstdint>

using namespace hexi::detail;

template<typename T>
std::size_t encode_varint(hexi::pmc::binary_stream_writer& writer, T value) {
    std::size_t bytes_written = 0;
    while (value > 0x7F) {
        const uint8_t byte = (value & 0x7F) | 0x80;
        writer.write(byte);
        value >>= 7;
        bytes_written++;
    }
    const uint8_t byte = value & 0x7F;
    writer.write(byte);
    return ++bytes_written;
}

template<typename T>
T decode_varint(hexi::pmc::binary_stream_reader& reader) {
    T result = 0;
    int shift = 0;
    uint8_t byte;
    do {
        byte = reader.read();
        result |= static_cast<T>(byte & 0x7F) << shift;
        shift += 7;
    } while (byte & 0x80);
    return result;
}

template<typename T>
bool encode_decode_roundtrip(T original_value) {
    using buf_type = std::vector<uint8_t>;
    hexi::pmc::buffer_adaptor<buf_type> buffer;
    hexi::pmc::binary_stream_writer writer(buffer);
    hexi::pmc::binary_stream_reader reader(buffer);

    encode_varint(writer, original_value);
    T decoded = decode_varint<T>(reader);
    return original_value == decoded;
}
```</answer>

<answer>```cpp
#include <hexi/hexi.h>
#include <string>
#include <string_view>

void write_string_prefixed_varint(hexi::binary_stream& stream, const std::string& str) {
    stream << hexi::prefixed_varint(str);
}

std::string read_string_prefixed_varint(hexi::binary_stream& stream) {
    std::string s;
    stream >> hexi::prefixed_varint(s);
    return s;
}
```</answer>

Hmm, each memory region can be represented as a start address and a length. For example, the source region starts at src and has a length of src_len, so its end is src + src_len. Similarly for the destination. Two regions overlap if one starts before the other ends and vice versa. Wait, but what about the case where one region is completely within another? Like, if src is [0-10] and dst is [5-15], they overlap. So the condition would be that the start of one is less than the end of the other, and the start of the other is less than the end of the first. Or maybe it's (src_start < dst_end && dst_start < src_end). That should cover all overlapping cases.

But wait, the regions are inclusive or exclusive? Because when you have a length, like src_len, the end is src + src_len. So the region includes up to that address. So for example, if src is at address 0 and length 5, the region is from 0 to 4 (assuming byte addressing). Wait, no, in C, when you have a pointer and a length, the region is from the pointer to pointer + length - 1. But maybe the problem considers the region as [start, start + len), which is the standard C convention. So the end is exclusive. So the region is from src to src + src_len, not including src + src_len. So the end is src + src_len. So for overlap, the condition is that the start of one is less than the end of the other, and the start of the other is less than the end of the first. So (src < dst + dst_len) && (dst < src + src_len). That should work.

So the steps are: convert the pointers to uintptr_t to compare their addresses. Then check if the source region overlaps with the destination region. So:

bool check_regions_overlap(const void* src, std::size_t src_len, const void* dst, std::size_t dst_len) {
    auto src_start = reinterpret_cast<uintptr_t>(src);
    auto src_end = src_start + src_len;
    auto dst_start = reinterpret_cast<uintptr_t>(dst);
    auto dst_end = dst_start + dst_len;
    
    return (src_start < dst_end) && (dst_start < src_end);
}

Wait, but what if the regions are exactly adjacent? Like src_end == dst_start. Then they don't overlap. So the condition is correct.

Now for the second function, safe_memory_copy. The idea is to check if the source and destination regions overlap. If they do, we need to handle it carefully. Otherwise, we can use memcpy. But if they overlap, we need to decide the direction of copying. For example, if the source is before the destination and they overlap, we should copy from the end to the beginning to avoid overwriting source data before it's copied. Conversely, if the destination is before the source, we copy from beginning to end.

So the steps for safe_memory_copy:

1. Check if the regions overlap using check_regions_overlap. If not, use memcpy directly.

2. If they do overlap, determine the direction of copying.

   a. If source is before destination and they overlap, copy backwards.

   b. If destination is before source and they overlap, copy forwards.

   c. If they are the same region, just copy normally (but maybe memcpy can handle that).

But how to determine the direction? Let's see. Suppose src is before dst. So src_start < dst_start. If they overlap, then src_end > dst_start. So the overlapping region is from dst_start to src_end. So we need to copy from the end of the source towards the beginning to avoid overwriting.

Alternatively, if dst is before src, then we copy from beginning to end.

So the code would look something like this:

bool safe_memory_copy(void* destination, const void* source, std::size_t length) {
    // Check for overlap
    if (!check_regions_overlap(source, length, destination, length)) {
        std::memcpy(destination, source, length);
        return true;
    }
    
    // Determine direction
    auto src_start = reinterpret_cast<uintptr_t>(source);
    auto dst_start = reinterpret_cast<uintptr_t>(destination);
    
    if (src_start < dst_start) {
        // Copy backwards to avoid overwriting
        for (std::size_t i = length; i > 0; --i) {
            reinterpret_cast<char*>(destination)[i-1] = 
                reinterpret_cast<const char*>(source)[i-1];
        }
    } else if (dst_start < src_start) {
        // Copy forwards
        for (std::size_t i = 0; i < length; ++i) {
            reinterpret_cast<char*>(destination)[i] = 
                reinterpret_cast<const char*>(source)[i];
        }
    } else {
        // Same region, copy normally
        std::memcpy(destination, source, length);
    }
    
    return true;
}

Wait, but what if the regions are the same? Like source and destination are the same, but length is non-zero. Then memcpy would still work. But in that case, the overlap check would return true, but since src_start == dst_start, the direction check would go into the else clause and use memcpy. Which is correct.

But in the code above, when there's overlap, we check if src_start < dst_start. If yes, copy backwards. Else if dst_start < src_start, copy forwards. Else, same region, copy normally.

But in the case where src_start == dst_start, the else clause is triggered, and memcpy is used. Which is correct.

bool check_regions_overlap(const void* src, std::size_t src_len, const void* dst, std::size_t dst_len) {

        std::memcpy(destination, source, length);

    auto dst_start = reinterpret_cast<uintptr_t>(destination);

}

<answer>```cpp
#include <hexi/shared.h>
#include <cstring>
#include <cstddef>

using namespace hexi::detail;

bool check_regions_overlap(const void* src, std::size_t src_len, const void* dst, std::size_t dst_len) {
    auto src_start = reinterpret_cast<uintptr_t>(src);
    auto src_end = src_start + src_len;
    auto dst_start = reinterpret_cast<uintptr_t>(dst);
    auto dst_end = dst_start + dst_len;
    
    return (src_start < dst_end) && (dst_start < src_end);
}

bool safe_memory_copy(void* destination, const void* source, std::size_t length) {
    if (!check_regions_overlap(source, length, destination, length)) {
        std::memcpy(destination, source, length);
        return true;
    }
    
    auto src_start = reinterpret_cast<uintptr_t>(source);
    auto dst_start = reinterpret_cast<uintptr_t>(destination);
    
    if (src_start < dst_start) {
        // Copy backwards to avoid overwriting source data
        for (std::size_t i = length; i > 0; --i) {
            reinterpret_cast<char*>(destination)[i - 1] = 
                reinterpret_cast<const char*>(source)[i - 1];
        }
    } else if (dst_start < src_start) {
        // Copy forwards
        for (std::size_t i = 0; i < length; ++i) {
            reinterpret_cast<char*>(destination)[i] = 
                reinterpret_cast<const char*>(source)[i];
        }
    } else {
        // Same region, use memcpy
        std::memcpy(destination, source, length);
    }
    
    return true;
}
```</answer>

#include <hexi/shared.h>
#include <array>
#include <cstdint>
#include <type_traits>

using namespace hexi::detail;

template<std::size_t Size>
constexpr auto create_filled_array(std::uint8_t value) {
    return []<std::size_t... Is>(std::uint8_t val, std::index_sequence<Is...>) {
        return std::array<std::uint8_t, sizeof...(Is)>{(val)...};
    }(value, std::make_index_sequence<Size>{});
}

bool verify_filled_array(const auto& arr, std::uint8_t expected_value) {
    for (std::size_t i = 0; i < arr.size(); ++i) {
        if (arr[i] != expected_value) {
            return false;
        }
    }
    return true;
}
```</answer>

struct CompactPacket {
    uint32_t packet_id;
    std::vector<std::string> tags;
    std::string data;
};

std::vector<uint8_t> serialize_compact_packet(const CompactPacket& packet) {
    std::vector<uint8_t> buffer;
    hexi::stream_adaptor adaptor(buffer);
    
    // Serialize packet_id using varint (not prefixed, as per problem clarification)
    hexi::varint_adaptor<uint32_t> packet_id_adaptor;
    packet_id_adaptor.serialize(adaptor, packet.packet_id);
    
    // Serialize tags vector size using prefixed_varint
    hexi::prefixed_varint_adaptor<size_t> tags_size_adaptor;
    tags_size_adaptor.serialize(adaptor, packet.tags.size());
    
    // Serialize each tag
    for (const auto& tag : packet.tags) {
        hexi::prefixed_varint_adaptor<size_t> tag_len_adaptor;
        tag_len_adaptor.serialize(adaptor, tag.length());
        adaptor.write(tag.data(), tag.length());
    }
    
    // Serialize data string length using prefixed_varint
    hexi::prefixed_varint_adaptor<size_t> data_len_adaptor;
    data_len_adaptor.serialize(adaptor, packet.data.length());
    adaptor.write(packet.data.data(), packet.data.length());
    
    return buffer;
}

CompactPacket deserialize_compact_packet(const std::span<const uint8_t> buffer) {
    CompactPacket packet;
    hexi::stream_adaptor adaptor(buffer);
    
    // Deserialize packet_id using varint

        tag.resize(tag.length());
        adaptor.read(&tag[0], tag.length());

<answer>```cpp
#include <hexi/hexi.h>
#include <string>
#include <cstdint>
#include <vector>
#include <algorithm>

struct PaddedData {
    uint16_t header;
    std::string data;
    uint32_t footer;
};

std::vector<uint8_t> serialize_with_padding(const PaddedData& packet) {
    std::vector<uint8_t> buffer;
    
    // Reserve space for efficiency
    buffer.reserve(sizeof(packet.header) + sizeof(uint16_t) + packet.data.size() + 16 + sizeof(packet.footer));
    
    // Write header
    const uint8_t* header_bytes = reinterpret_cast<const uint8_t*>(&packet.header);
    buffer.insert(buffer.end(), header_bytes, header_bytes + sizeof(packet.header));
    
    // Write data length (uint16_t)
    uint16_t data_len = static_cast<uint16_t>(packet.data.size());
    const uint8_t* len_bytes = reinterpret_cast<const uint8_t*>(&data_len);
    buffer.insert(buffer.end(), len_bytes, len_bytes + sizeof(data_len));
    
    // Write data string
    buffer.insert(buffer.end(), packet.data.begin(), packet.data.end());
    
    // Write 16 bytes of padding (0x00) using fill<16>(0x00)
    auto padding = hexi::fill<16>(0x00);
    buffer.insert(buffer.end(), padding.begin(), padding.end());
    
    // Write footer
    const uint8_t* footer_bytes = reinterpret_cast<const uint8_t*>(&packet.footer);
    buffer.insert(buffer.end(), footer_bytes, footer_bytes + sizeof(packet.footer));
    
    return buffer;
}
```</answer>

output.text:
<answer>```cpp
#include <hexi/allocators/block_allocator.h>
#include <vector>
#include <memory>

struct PoolObject {
    int id;
    double value;
    
    PoolObject() : id(0), value(0.0) {}
    PoolObject(int i, double v) : id(i), value(v) {}
};

std::vector<PoolObject*> create_object_pool(hexi::block_allocator<PoolObject, 10>& allocator) {
    std::vector<PoolObject*> objects;
    
    // Allocate and construct three objects with different values
    void* mem1 = allocator.allocate(1);
    new(mem1) PoolObject(1, 1.1);
    objects.push_back(static_cast<PoolObject*>(mem1));
    
    void* mem2 = allocator.allocate(1);
    new(mem2) PoolObject(2, 2.2);
    objects.push_back(static_cast<PoolObject*>(mem2));
    
    void* mem3 = allocator.allocate(1);
    new(mem3) PoolObject(3, 3.3);
    objects.push_back(static_cast<PoolObject*>(mem3));
    
    return objects;
}
```</answer>

<answer>```cpp
#include <hexi/pmc/buffer_base.h>
#include <vector>
#include <cstddef>

class CustomBuffer : public hexi::pmc::buffer_base {
    std::vector<std::byte> data_;
    
public:
    std::size_t size() const override {
        return data_.size();
    }
    
    bool empty() const override {
        return data_.empty();
    }
    
    // Helper method to add data (for testing)
    void add_data(const void* source, std::size_t length) {
        const std::byte* bytes = static_cast<const std::byte*>(source);
        data_.insert(data_.end(), bytes, bytes + length);
    }
    
    // Helper method to clear data (for testing)
    void clear() {
        data_.clear();
    }
};
```</answer>

    std::vector<uint8_t> result;
    result.resize(read_adaptor.size());
    read_adaptor.read(result.data(), result.size());
    
    return result;
}

std::vector<uint8_t> peek_data_without_advancing(const std::vector<uint8_t>& buffer_data, std::size_t peek_size) {
    hexi::pmc::buffer buffer(buffer_data);
    hexi::pmc::buffer_read_adaptor read_adaptor(buffer);
    
    // Peek at data without advancing the read pointer
    std::vector<uint8_t> peeked_data(peek_size);
    read_adaptor.copy(peeked_data.data(), peek_size);
    
    // Get current read position pointer
    const uint8_t* read_ptr = read_adaptor.read_ptr();
    
    // Consume the peeked data
    std::vector<uint8_t> consumed_data(peek_size);
    read_adaptor.read(consumed_data.data(), peek_size);
    
    return peeked_data;
}

<answer>```cpp
#include <hexi/pmc/buffer_read_adaptor.h>
#include <hexi/pmc/buffer_write_adaptor.h>
#include <vector>
#include <cstdint>

struct TypedData {
    uint32_t id;
    int16_t value;
    double ratio;
};

std::vector<uint8_t> write_typed_data(const TypedData& data) {
    std::vector<uint8_t> buffer;
    hexi::pmc::buffer_write_adaptor adaptor(buffer);
    adaptor.write(data.id);
    adaptor.write(data.value);
    adaptor.write(data.ratio);
    return buffer;
}

TypedData read_typed_data(const std::vector<uint8_t>& buffer) {
    hexi::pmc::buffer_read_adaptor adaptor(buffer);
    TypedData data;
    adaptor.read(&data.id);
    adaptor.read(&data.value);
    adaptor.read(&data.ratio);
    return data;
}
```</answer>

#include <hexi/hexi.h>
#include <hexi/endian.h>
#include <vector>
#include <cstdint>

struct ProtocolPacket {
    uint32_t message_id;
    std::vector<uint16_t> data_values;
    uint32_t checksum;
};

std::vector<uint8_t> serialize_protocol_packet(const ProtocolPacket& packet) {
    hexi::binary_stream stream;
    
    // Convert message_id to big-endian and write
    auto be_msg_id = hexi::endian::native_to_big(packet.message_id);
    stream.write(be_msg_id);
    
    // Convert each data_value to big-endian and write the container
    std::vector<uint16_t> be_data_values;
    for (auto val : packet.data_values) {
        be_data_values.push_back(hexi::endian::native_to_big(val));
    }
    stream.write_container(be_data_values);
    
    // Convert checksum to big-endian and write
    auto be_checksum = hexi::endian::native_to_big(packet.checksum);
    stream.write(be_checksum);
    
    return stream.get_buffer();
}

ProtocolPacket deserialize_protocol_packet(const std::span<const uint8_t> buffer) {
    hexi::binary_stream stream(buffer);
    ProtocolPacket packet;
    
    // Read message_id and convert from big-endian to native
    uint32_t be_msg_id;
    stream.read(be_msg_id);
    packet.message_id = hexi::endian::big_to_native(be_msg_id);
    
    // Read data_values count and convert from big-endian to native
    uint32_t count_be;
    stream.read(count_be);
    uint32_t count = hexi::endian::big_to_native(count_be);
    
    // Read data_values and convert each element from big-endian to native
    std::vector<uint16_t> data_values;
    stream.read_container(data_values, count);
    for (auto& val : data_values) {
        val = hexi::endian::big_to_native(val);
    }
    packet.data_values = std::move(data_values);
    
    // Read checksum and convert from big-endian to native
    uint32_t be_checksum;
    stream.read(be_checksum);
    packet.checksum = hexi::endian::big_to_native(be_checksum);
    
    return packet;
}

struct ProtocolHeader {
    uint32_t message_id;
};

ProtocolHeader extract_protocol_header(const std::span<const uint8_t> buffer) {
    // Extract the first 4 bytes as big-endian uint32_t
    uint32_t be_message_id = (static_cast<uint32_t>(buffer[0]) << 24) |
                             (static_cast<uint32_t>(buffer[1]) << 16) |
                             (static_cast<uint32_t>(buffer[2]) << 8) |
                             static_cast<uint32_t>(buffer[3]);
    
    // Convert to native endianness
    uint32_t native_id = hexi::endian::big_to_native(be_message_id);
    
    return {native_id};
}
```</answer>

#include <hexi/hexi.h>
#include <hexi/endian.h>

struct MeasurementData {
    uint32_t sensor_id;
    std::vector<float> temperatures;
    double pressure;
    
    void serialise(auto& adaptor) const {
        // Convert sensor_id to little-endian before writing
        auto converted_id = hexi::conditional_reverse(sensor_id, std::endian::native, std::endian::little);
        adaptor.write(&converted_id, sizeof(converted_id));
        
        // Write each temperature converted to little-endian
        for (float temp : temperatures) {
            auto converted_temp = hexi::conditional_reverse(temp, std::endian::native, std::endian::little);
            adaptor.write(&converted_temp, sizeof(converted_temp));
        }
        
        // Convert pressure to little-endian before writing
        auto converted_pressure = hexi::conditional_reverse(pressure, std::endian::native, std::endian::little);
        adaptor.write(&converted_pressure, sizeof(converted_pressure));
    }
    
    void deserialise(auto& adaptor) {
        // Read sensor_id (written as little-endian) and convert to native
        uint32_t raw_id;
        adaptor.read(&raw_id, sizeof(raw_id));
        sensor_id = hexi::little_to_native_inplace(raw_id);
        
        // Read each temperature (written as little-endian) and convert to native
        temperatures.clear();
        float temp;
        while (adaptor.read(&temp, sizeof(temp))) {
            temperatures.push_back(hexi::little_to_native_inplace(temp));
        }
        
        // Read pressure (written as little-endian) and convert to native
        double raw_pressure;
        adaptor.read(&raw_pressure, sizeof(raw_pressure));
        pressure = hexi::little_to_native_inplace(raw_pressure);
    }
};

std::vector<uint8_t> serialize_measurement_data(const MeasurementData& data) {
    hexi::allocators::block_allocator allocator;
    hexi::stream_adaptor adaptor(allocator);
    data.serialise(adaptor);
    return std::vector<uint8_t>(adaptor.data(), adaptor.data() + adaptor.size());
}

MeasurementData deserialize_measurement_data(const std::span<const uint8_t> buffer) {
    hexi::allocators::block_allocator allocator;
    hexi::stream_adaptor adaptor(allocator);
    adaptor.write(buffer.data(), buffer.size());
    MeasurementData data;
    data.deserialise(adaptor);
    return data;
}

struct SensorInfo {
    uint32_t sensor_id;
    std::string_view sensor_name;
};

SensorInfo extract_sensor_info(const std::span<const uint8_t> buffer) {
    hexi::allocators::block_allocator allocator;
    hexi::stream_adaptor adaptor(allocator);
    adaptor.write(buffer.data(), buffer.size());
    
    // Extract null-terminated sensor name
    auto view_result = adaptor.view();
    std::string_view sensor_name = view_result.str;
    
    // Enforce read bounds for sensor_id
    adaptor.enforce_read_bounds(sizeof(uint32_t));
    
    // Read sensor_id (little-endian) and convert to native
    uint32_t raw_id;
    adaptor.read(&raw_id, sizeof(raw_id));
    uint32_t sensor_id = hexi::little_to_native_inplace(raw_id);
    
    // Skip remaining data
    adaptor.skip(adaptor.remaining());
    
    return {sensor_id, sensor_name};
}
